description: Fix confirmed CLI bugs: bare npm spawn calls that break on Windows, --show-config failing before first start, stale browser tool count in README, unvalidated browser tool names in config set, and improved update command UX with server type detection and auto-restart.

userStories.title: Use platformExec for all npm spawn calls in plugin.ts and update.ts
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want all npm spawn calls to use platformExec('npm') so that the CLI works on Windows where npm.cmd is required.
acceptanceCriteria: All spawn('npm', ...) calls in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts use platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm'
All spawnSync('npm', ...) calls in platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts use platformExec('npm') instead of bare 'npm'
userStories.notes: In platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts: three call sites use bare 'npm' — packageExistsOnNpmAsync (~line 92), fetchPackageInfo (~line 282), and npmSearchPlugins (~line 348). The file already imports platformExec from @opentabs-dev/shared and uses it in other places (lines 162, 236, 244, 518). Just change the three bare 'npm' to platformExec('npm'). In platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts: two spawnSync calls use bare 'npm' — getLatestVersion (~line 34) and performUpdate (~line 58). The file does NOT import platformExec — add the import from @opentabs-dev/shared.

userStories.title: Make --show-config work before first opentabs start
userStories.description: As a user, I want opentabs start --show-config to work even if I haven't run opentabs start yet, so I can preview the MCP configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs start --show-config does not error when ~/.opentabs/ does not exist
If auth secret doesn't exist, it is generated on the fly (or a placeholder is shown)
The MCP JSON config is still printed correctly
userStories.notes: In platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts around line 280-289: when options.showConfig is true and readAuthSecret() returns null, it currently prints an error and exits. Instead, generate the secret (call ensureAuthSecret or equivalent) before printing the config. Look at the normal start flow to see how the secret is initialized — reuse that logic. The key insight is that --show-config should be self-contained: it should ensure all prerequisites exist, print the config, and exit.

userStories.title: Update stale browser tool count in README.md
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want the README to reflect the correct browser tool count.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md browser tool count matches the actual count in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts
userStories.notes: Count the actual number of browser tools exported from the browserTools array in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts (currently 40). Update README.md — search for the old count (likely '39') and replace with the correct number. There may be multiple occurrences (check lines ~31 and ~84). Also check docs/ pages that mention the count.

userStories.title: Reject unrecognized browser tool names before writing config
userStories.description: As a user, I want opentabs config set browser-tool to reject invalid tool names when the server is running, so I don't silently create bad config entries.
acceptanceCriteria: When the server is running and the tool name is not in the registered list, the command exits with an error WITHOUT writing the config
When the server is not running, the command still writes the config with a warning (existing behavior)
userStories.notes: In platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, handleSetBrowserTool function (~lines 400-452). Currently the validation happens AFTER the config is written to disk (~line 433). Move the fetchBrowserToolNames check BEFORE the writeConfig call. If the server responds and the tool name is not in the list, print the error and exit 1 without writing. If the server is not reachable (fetch fails), fall through to writing with the existing warning. The BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG from @opentabs-dev/shared could also be used for offline validation as a fallback.

userStories.title: Detect server type and display running version during update
userStories.description: As a user, I want the update command to show the running server version and detect whether it's a background daemon, foreground process, or dev mode server.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs update displays the running server's version before updating (read from /health response header x-opentabs-version)
The update command detects the server type: background (PID file exists), dev (mode field from health endpoint), or foreground
userStories.notes: In platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts: the isServerRunning function (~line 44-53) already fetches /health. Change it to also read res.headers.get('x-opentabs-version') and the response body's mode field ('dev' or 'production'). Return an object { running: boolean, version?: string, mode?: string } instead of a bare boolean. For background detection, check if a PID file exists — look at platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts for the PID file reading pattern (getPidFilePath). A server is 'background' if the PID file exists and the process is alive, 'dev' if mode === 'dev', otherwise 'foreground'.

userStories.title: Auto-restart background servers and tailor post-update messages
userStories.description: As a user, I want the update command to auto-restart background daemons and give me context-appropriate restart instructions for foreground/dev servers.
acceptanceCriteria: After update succeeds and server type is background: the old server is stopped and a new one is started automatically
After update succeeds and server type is foreground: message says to Ctrl+C and run opentabs start
After update succeeds and server type is dev: message says to restart npm run dev
After update succeeds and no server is running: no restart message
userStories.notes: In platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts: replace the generic restart block (~lines 120-125) with a switch on server type from US-005. For background: SIGTERM the PID, poll for exit (reuse waitForExit pattern from stop.ts), then spawn opentabs start --background. For foreground: print 'Stop the server (Ctrl+C) and run: opentabs start'. For dev: print 'Restart your dev server (npm run dev)'. For not running: just print 'Update complete.' This depends on US-005's server type detection.
